cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking for the writer's opinion of the story, so I need to figure out which of the given options best represents the main message or moral of the story. The story is about a guy who has cancer and doesn't know how long he has left. He becomes friends with a girl at a music store and buys a CD every day, but he's too shy to ask her out. He leaves his phone number once, but before she can respond, he dies. The mother finds out all the CDs had notes from the girl saying she wanted to go out with him, but he never got the chance to know. The key point here is that the guy didn't express his feelings or take action until it was too late. The story's message seems to be about not waiting to show your feelings because life is uncertain. Let me look at the options: (A) Don't wait to show your feelings. (B) Life always goes like that. (C) There is only one chance for everyone. (D) Show your feelings in a proper way. Option A seems to fit because the story emphasizes that the guy didn't act on his feelings until it was too late. The writer is likely advising people to not delay expressing their feelings. Option B is a bit vague. "Life always goes like that" could be true, but it's not as specific as the other options. The story is more about taking action than just the general nature of life. Option C says there's only one chance for everyone. While the guy had one chance to ask the girl out, the story's main message isn't about the number of chances but about acting in time. The CD's notes were there, but he didn't get to respond until after his death. So maybe the point is that you should act on opportunities when they come, not that you have only one chance.
